findings.name: Dockerfile base image not digest-pinned
findings.detail: FROM node:24 uses a tag without digest pinning. Builder image can be silently substituted.

findings.name: Dockerfile final stage not digest-pinned
findings.detail: FROM gcr.io/distroless/nodejs24-debian13 uses no tag and no digest.

findings.name: Dockerfile sets non-root user (65532)
findings.detail: USER 65532 correctly sets non-root execution context.

findings.name: Docker Compose uses :latest tag
findings.detail: image: bkimminich/juice-shop:latest — mutable tag, not reproducible.
findings.remediation: Pin to specific version tag or digest

findings.name: GitHub Actions use unpinned version tags
findings.detail: 3 CodeQL actions use @v3 tag not @sha256 digest. Supply chain substitution possible.
findings.remediation: Pin all third-party actions to SHA digest

findings.name: Dependabot intentionally ignores vulnerable dependencies
findings.detail: express-jwt@0.1.3, sanitize-html@1.4.2, unzipper@0.9.15 explicitly suppressed from auto-update. These are known-vulnerable versions retained intentionally as training artifacts.
findings.remediation: By design for training — do not change in production-equivalent deployments

findings.name: GitHub Actions permissions not least-privilege
findings.detail: No top-level permissions block found. Default token permissions may be overly broad (read access to all scopes for GITHUB_TOKEN).
findings.remediation: Add permissions: read-all at workflow level or restrict per-job
